comments.issue: Overly permissive query exposing all columns
comments.suggestion: Specify only necessary columns in the SELECT statement to reduce data exposure

comments.issue: Lack of input validation and parameterization
comments.suggestion: Consider using parameterized queries or prepared statements to improve security, although no user input is visible in this snippet

security_report.summary: No critical security vulnerabilities found, but potential for improvement in query structure and data exposure

comments.suggestion: Specify only the required columns in the SELECT statement
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of comma-separated tables

comments.issue: Function applied to column in WHERE clause (bypasses indexes)
comments.suggestion: Consider using a date range instead of the YEAR function, e.g., created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND created_at < '2025-01-01'

comments.issue: ORDER BY without LIMIT on large datasets
comments.suggestion: Consider adding a LIMIT clause to restrict the number of rows returned

performance_report.summary: Severe performance issues due to Cartesian join, implicit join, and function applied to column in WHERE clause

comments.issue: Inconsistent keyword casing (YEAR should be UPPER_CASE)
comments.suggestion: Replace YEAR with UPPER_CASE YEAR() function
Use table aliases (e.g., u for users, o for orders)

comments.issue: Deep nesting could be improved with CTEs or subqueries
comments.suggestion: Consider breaking down the query into smaller, more manageable parts

style_report.summary: SQL query has inconsistent keyword casing and missing table aliasing

critical_issues: Function applied to column in WHERE clause (bypasses indexes)
report.summary: Security: No critical security vulnerabilities found, but potential for improvement in query structure and data exposure | Performance: Severe performance issues due to Cartesian join, implicit join, and function applied to column in WHERE clause | Style: SQL query has inconsistent keyword casing and missing table aliasing
